samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit full adder in combinational logic. 16-bit input operand A. 16-bit input operand B. Carry-in input. 16-bit sum output. Carry-out output.

Design a 16-bit full adder using a combination of half adders and full adders.

First, design a half adder.

Half adder:

module half_adder ( input wire a, input wire b, output wire sum, output wire cout ); assign sum = a ^ b; assign cout = a & b; endmodule

Next, design a full adder.

Full adder:

module full_adder ( input wire a, input wire b, input wire cin, output wire sum, output wire cout ); wire ha_sum; wire ha_cout1; wire ha_cout2; half_adder ha1 ( .a(a), .b(b), .sum(ha_sum), .cout(ha_cout1) ); half_adder ha2 ( .a(ha_sum), .b(cin), .sum(sum), .cout(ha_cout2) ); assign cout = ha_cout1 | ha_cout2; endmodule

Finally, design a 16-bit full adder using the half adder and full adder. The first bit can use a half adder, and the remaining bits should use full adders. The carry-out of each adder should be connected to the carry-in of the next adder.

16-bit Full adder:

module adder_16bit ( input wire [15:0] a, input wire [15:0] b, input wire cin, output wire [15:0] sum, output wire cout ); wire [15:0] c; half_adder ha ( .a(a[0]), .b(b[0]), .sum(sum[0]), .cout(c[0]) ); genvar i; generate for (i = 1; i < 16; i = i + 1) begin : gen_adders full_adder fa ( .a(a[i]), .b(b[i]), .cin(c[i-1]), .sum(sum[i]), .cout(c[i]) ); end endgenerate assign cout = c[15]; endmodule
